text:
Person1: Our friends in Little Russia.  Go in and ask for a man named Dmitri.
Person2: Dmitri?
Person1: The man who hired me for this contract.
Person2: I don't get it.
Person1: You're gonna be me.  You're gonna go in, and you're gonna get the info on the remaining two hits.
Person2: Why me?  Why don't you do it?
Person1: No client has ever seen my face, and I intend to keep it that way.  Besides, if he decides to put a bullet in my head, I don't wanna be there for it.
Person2: He's gonna shoot me?
Person1: When he finds out you tossed his list? I would.
Person2: No.  No way.  I can't do this.
Person1: Max.  You threw my briefcase in the river.  You've got balls bigger than Toledo.
Person2: I...I wasn't thinking.  I just did it.
Person1: That's jazz, my friend.  You said it yourself.  So don't tell me you don't know how to play between the notes.

Person1: Wait I...
Person2: You were paid to take me out!  By -- the one person I truly hate.  I knew it was a set-up!
Person1: It wasn't like that.
Person2: Really?  What was it like?  A down payment now, then a bonus for sleeping with me?
Person1: I didn't care about the money.

Person1: They found another note?
Person2: Uh-huh. Another News clipping. Another one about Sid's mom. Dewey, whether we like it  or not it looks like we're going to have to  go back into our past.
Person1: You mean OUR past as in Me and Sid. This has nothing to do with you Gale.
Person2: This has everything to do with me Dewey. I was the one who brought the nation's attention to this shit anyway. I'll be damned if I'm gonna sit around and pass on the buck again.
Person1: Do you really mean it this time Gale?
Person2: Yes. Look, let's get something straight Dewey, I got into this shit cause the police came looking for ME. Not cause I'm out to make the SUNRISE STUDIOS SLASHINGS.
Person1: Why would the police come to you?
Person2: Again, I am the author of the definitive book on the Woodsboro murders.!  What about you?
Person1: What about me?
Person2: You said you'd never leave Woodsboro, 'The only place that's real!'  But now you're here. Not with me.  Dewey, I took care of you.  I waited until you were well.  I couldn't stay there, I mean, it's like dog years, one year in Woodsboro is like seven everywhere else.
Person1: So it's off to London for a week?  New York for a month? L.A Forever?
Person2: It was '60 Minutes II'!  I couldn't say no!  I could've been the next Diane Sawyer!
Person1: What's wrong with just being Gale Weathers? I liked her!
Person2: It didn't work Dewey.  We tried,  we're different.
Person1: You used to say that was our strength.
Person2: Dewey, you're not just here because of that second rate K-Mart, straight-to-video version of me, are you?
Person1: Brace yourself, Gale, all of this is actually not About you.
Person2: All of what Dewey?  You do know something, don't you?
Person1: Off the record.
Person2: Always.
Person1: Two months ago, the Woodsboro police got a call from a woman who said she was with 'Stab 3'.  She wanted to see the file on Sid, for research.
Person2: And?
Person1: The boys said no.  She wouldn't give her name. A month later, the station gets broken into, the file room ransacked.
Person2: Someone stole Sid's file?
Person1: I already removed it.  I think someone on the film was trying to find Sid, and that's the only reason I came here. To make do a little private investigating of my own and make sure no one here was planning anything.
Person2: I'm sorry, this is too good!
Person1: What part of 'Off the record' don't you understand?
Person2: Right. Sorry.  Do you think he did it?
Person1: I've got my suspicions but still, nothing  you say can ever make me take my eyes off one person.
Person2: And who is that?

Person1: If you don't eat that ice cream right now, I'm gonna strangle you until you are completely dead.
Person2: Yeah? You and what army?
Person1: Next thing, you're gonna be complaining about the seats.
Person2: Well, if you must know, they could be closer.
Person1: Damn, I shoulda let Spanky Johnson drown you in the river when I had the chance.

Person1: I don't know what kind of game you're playing here but I'm out because you are a whack-job.
Person2: He was working on something called Project Manticore, which was using recombinant DNA to produce a superior human...a warrior...an advanced infantry soldier.
Person1: Not that I don't enjoy a good urban legend now and then but what does any of this have to do with me?
Person2: The bar code on your neck, Max.  I know who you are and I know who you're running from.

Person1: It's OK. There's no problem.
Person2: You mean I can have the room?
Person1: Well, that's what I said, isn't it?

Person1: Nobody wants any more killings; we all agree to that, correct?  That's good.  Now, Eric, you're gonna hand your prisoner over to us.
Person2: Screw you.
Person1: This isn't your concern.  It's over, here and now.

Person1: Is that my -- scenario?  Tell me.
Person2: They'd frame him.
Person1: He's out of prison?
Person2: They're already watching you. If they had to, they'd give him this drug that mimics an alcoholic blackout. He'd wake up not even remembering his "act of revenge."

Person1: You're alive! She didn't kill you!
Person2: Who didn't?
Person1: That woman who's after me. She hates you. Because she knows I love you.
Person2: You love me?
Person1: My angel. My goddess. I feel I've always loved you all my life. I'll die if I can't spend the rest of my life just looking at you, holding you in my arms...
Person2: We have to have a long talk about all this.
Person1: I know just the place. Hang a left at the Amalgamated Tower.

Person1: Don't be a good neighbor to her, or I'm going to send you a heartfelt letter, straight from my heart, buddy. You know what a heartfelt letter is? It's a message. straight from my thoughts, buddy. Once you get a heartfelt letter from me, you're in trouble forever. Understand, buddy?
Person2: Yes.
Person1: I'll send you on a difficult journey, buddy!

Person1: I've seen lots of people.  It's not a big thing for me.
Person2: Hand me a towel.  I'm getting out.
Person1: I'm coming in.
Person2: I don't want you to!
Person1: We're not gonna do anything.
Person2: Why are you so stupid?
Person1: Why are you so uptight?
Person2: I don't even...what do you mean?  I don't even know what that means.
Person1: It means I'm gonna shampoo my hair and stay out of your way.
Person2: Hand me a towel.  Hand me a towel.

Person1: Y'know, you got an awful short memory for direct orders. I told you to stay beside me.
Person2: -- C'mon, Stephen.
Person1: -- You split the team, man. And what was that crap with the standpipe? You'd think you and a hose were never introduced before.

Person1: And then, there's Richard's Burrito.
Person2: What's that?
Person1: That's my own theory based on a Mexican food called the burrito. I had it once when my parents took me to Disneyland.
Person2: I know what a burrito is.
Person1: Well, I think time is like a burrito. Sometimes it just folds over on itself and one part touches the other.
Person2: What's inside?
Person1: You can till it with whatever you want. From illusions to memory, from experience to innocence, from happiness to the entire universes
Person2: So you think time travel is possible? For people?
Person1: Absolutely. People, dogs, elephants.
Person2: Listen, you've gotta keep this a secret. You can't tell a soul. Promise?
Person1: Okay. I promise.
Person2: This is serious. Nobody can know. Ah, I've returned from the future. I traveled back here 25 years.
Person1: You probably are crazy. Wait a minute. Is this some kind of joke? I know what you all, think of me.

Person1: You mean we get `Dave' to nominate you as Vice President.
Person2: I was a senator, you know.
Person1: Oh, I know. And then when our poor President gets another stroke - - of course much more serious this time - - the newly appointed V.P. becomes the Pres...
Person2: What about containment, Alan?

Person1: I think it's contaminated.
Person2: You don't even know what that word means.

Person1: And I couldn't follow her.  God knows I tried.  One doesn't often get a second chance. I want to stop being haunted. You're my second chances, Judy.
Person2: Take me away...
Person1: You look like Madeleine, now. Go up the stairs.
Person2: No!
Person1: Go up the stairs, Judy.  I'll follow.

Person1: After all I've done, I can’t believe you’re doubting my motives!
Person2: It's just hard to believe you suddenly care when you've been distant for so long.
Person1: I've been going through things, Mia. That doesn't mean I don't care.
Person2: I need consistent effort, Eli, not just moments of clarity.

Person1: I've come to strike a bargain with you.  I wish to learn the power of changing.
Person2: And what would'st I gain from this bargain?
Person1: Your life.
Person2: How?

Person1: I did not get the report from an MI-6 agent.
Person2: Who did you get it from?
Person1: I am just a middle man. I am doing the honourable thing and returning the money to its rightful owner...
Person2: And we know how difficult that can be for the Swiss.
Person1: Your last chance. Take the money.
Person2: Your last chance. Give me the name.
Person1: Stripped of all your weaponry, you still threaten me?

Person1: Are you okay?  I wish I hadn't told you what I did.  But I can't help the way I feel.  You don't hold that against me, do you?
Person2: No.  That wouldn't be fair.  Where are we going, Jerry?
Person1: It would be a lot easier for me to show you instead of tell you.  But first things first.

Person1: Merde... Wait here. When I return you tell me about last night, huh?
Person2: But... I don't remember anything, I swear...
Person1: I leave these open. Maybe something comes back to you.

Person1: Bob was a grown man.  In any great struggle, there will be casualties. Wouldn't that be implicit in the name?  Project "Mayhem."
Person2: Forget your struggle.  I want out.
Person1: You want out?
Person2: I quit.
Person1: Not an option, for the most obvious of reasons.  You need to get with the program.  Seven minutes.  Let's get out of here.

Person1: Dad, please stop.  Please.  I'm sure I know what you're talking about.
Person2: Sure you know, son, but I think you've been having a little problem with it. It's okay, though.  What you're doing is perfectly normal.  It's like practice.  Like when you play tennis against a wall.  Some day, there'll be a partner returning the ball.  You do want a partner, don't you son?
Person1: Yes.
Person2: That's great.  Now remember, it's okay to play with yourself.  Or, as I always called it --  "Stroke the salami!"  Ho-ho, Jim.  There's nothing to be ashamed of.  Hell, I'm fifty-two, and I still enjoy masturbating.  Uncle Mort masturbates.  We all masturbate.

Person1: There was another call too. From your agent. He says your summer stock deal is all set. So you're going back east, huh?
Person2: ...Yes.
Person1: Without me.
Person2: Showdown.
Person1: You weren't going to tell me, were you? Just pack up and leave, is that it?
Person2: Dick, we need some time apart --
Person1: Hey, I'm not a complete fool, you know. I can see you're going sour on me, and when I try to do something about it, you turn your back and say it's nothing.
Person2: Dick, I can't even breathe here...
Person1: Dwayne! I'm Dwayne now! And you damn well better get used to it!
Person2: Dick...
Person1: I don't suppose it occurred to you that I might want to leave too, that I might want to do theatre? No, 'cause you don't want me along, do you? And the reason has nothing to do with summer stock.
Person2: No?
Person1: No. It's all about that night, isn't it?
Person2: What night?
Person1: The premiere. I never pressed you about it but god damn it, you're gonna tell me right here and right now what happened and where the hell you were!
Person2: You want his name?

Person1: Larry, I'm gonna kill him.
Person2: Goddamn you, Joe, don't make me do this!
Person1: Larry, I'm askin' you to trust me on this.
Person2: Don't ask me that.
Person1: I'm not askin', I'm betting.

Person1: Pain by nerve induction... A human can resist any pain. Our test is crisis and observation.
Person2: I see the truth of it. -- REVEREND MOTHER  Could he be the one?... Maybe... but will he be ours to control?  You know when people speak the truth?

Person1: Well, uh...
Person2: Sure, sure, that doesn't count. That's different.
Person1: Well, it doesn't matter, does it? I'm getting out.
Person2: And that's why you've got to get rid of this money. If you keep it around, it'll just make you think how clever you are. It'll be a temptation to get back into the game.
Person1: Oh, that's it! You're stealing my money for my own good! How very motherly of you, Lilly.

Person1: What's wrong with you?
Person2: You ready for this? I've recently discovered that our good friend Mrs. Caldwell is the one who sent the letter to Annette urging her to stay away from me.
Person1: Interesting.
Person2: I now plan to devote all my energies to destroying the douche bag. Any luck corrupting her daughter?
Person1: No.
Person2: Call Cecile up and get her to come over. I'll bust that cherry in a heartbeat.

Person1: Why is it, do you think, that of all the professions in the world... you chose protection?
Person2: Are you for real?
Person1: You could have poured coffee in Starbucks, you could have learned to install track lighting in office buildings, you could have told people their horoscopes on the internet... You could have been one of ten thousand things... but in the end, you chose to protect people. You made that decision... and I find that very, very interesting.  Now all I need is your credit card number.

Person1: I'm sixteen, Keri.  I should be able to live wherever I want.
Person2: And I should have a son who calls me "Mom".  Looks like we're both shit out of luck.
Person1: Okay, you win.  I'll call you Mom. Now can I move into the dorms?
Person2: No.

Person1: I just hate to see you pining away. It makes me want to vomit, frankly.  Broken hearts are for assholes.
Person2: Hey, Iris, you okay?

Person1: Oh my God, does this mean you're becoming normal?
Person2: It means that Gigglepuss is playing at Club Skunk and we're going.
Person1: Oh, I thought you might have a date  I don't know why I'm bothering to ask, but are you going to Bogey Lowenstein's party Saturday night?
Person2: What do you think?
Person1: I think you're a freak.  I think you do this to torture me.  And I think you suck.

Person1: You got any money?
Person2: Thought you had a man for that.
Person1: I said we wasn't gonna talk about him.
Person2: What we just did, you askin' for money, make a man stop. I ain't callin' you no ho. But I ain't gonna be played like no trick, neither. Remember... you called me.
Person1: Save that hustle talk to them field ballers you sell crack to.
Person2: What'd I tell you?  I don't do none of that shit no more. I'm in communications now.
Person1: Stolen phone cards and two-ways is what you sayin'.

Person1: I want you to give up your work....
Person2: Mom, please don't start that again....
Person1: You could teach...You could....
Person2: I'm not going to quit now....
Person1: You're all I have left...I don't want you going in the water....

Person1: I should kill you right here! I never thought you'd do this to me but I guess there's a lot I didn't know about you!
Person2: Dwayne listen to me -
Person1: Shut up!  Not another word out of you!  You're going down, the Feds know all about it!  You didn't know some of it was marked, did you! Where's the rest?
Person2: I don't know what you're -
Person1: Don't lie to me!!!!

Person1: Forget about me.  You have enough problems of your own.
Person2: ...Do you really want me to forget about you?
Person1: I don't want to drag you down with me.
Person2: Daphne, I...

Person1: Get out.
Person2: Wait, hold on, Mary -- it's not as bad as it sounds. I certainly didn't know --
Person1: That you put a murderer on my trail?
Person2: Well yeah, I didn't know much about him. I just thought --
Person1: What did you think, Ted? That you could spy on me and trick me into thinking you were someone I could... really go for?

Person1: Now I didn't mean that, you rat.
Person2: Two weeks from tomorrow.
Person1: You're going?
Person2: You're going.

Person1: You know, this isn't gonna end well, these things never do.
Person2: Yeah, well, if it turns out I get shot like a dog, it'll be in the street, not off a goddamn fence.
Person1: You must see yourself as some kind of Clyde Barrow.

Person1: I've been so lonely.
Person2: I can't tell you how not interested I am.
Person1: Don't be nervous.  They said you'd be nervous.
Person2: Don't take another step.

Person1: I still ask, why do they want disarmament?
Person2: Well, for the same reasons we do, sir. Don't you see?
Person1: No, Major, I don't.  They have no regard for human life.  They wouldn't care if they lost their whole country as long as they won.
Person2: Gee, sir, that last remark doesn't exactly make all the sense in the world.
Person1: Major, you're talking like one of them!
Person2: Well, I'm not, sir.  Honestly, sir.
Person1: Don't be offended, Major.  Our President holds the same views.
Person2: Don't you think he knows something about this, General Ripper?
Person1: I'll tell you what I do think.  If they say they're for disarmament, I say anyone who says they're for disarmament is either a traitor or a damned fool.
Person2: But, General, we're on our toes.  We haven't agreed to anything for years.  Inffact, a a lot of people say we never will.
Person1: But if they suddenly opened up and gave us the inspection we want, we'd agree, wouldn't we?
Person2: If they gave us what we think we need, yes, I guess we would.
Person1: And you'd like to see that?
Person2: General, what's good enough for the President and all the experts he's got working on the thing, is good enough for me.
Person1: Do you think we'd cheat?
Person2: No, sir.  I'm sure we wouldn't.
Person1: Do you think they would cheat?
Person2: Look, sir.  I'm no expert on the subject, but I've read some pretty sharp ideas the big boys have.  Like, say, both countries agreeing to a million dollar reward and international protection for anyone who gives evidence of cheating to the inspectors. You can't hide those things without a lot of people knowing about it.  And if I were going to try and hide a few, I wouldn't want to depend on the fact that some poor slob isn't going to run and blab for a million bucks.  We're as smart as they are, and if they cheat, or even hold back information, we'd pull right out.
Person1: Major, I hate to say this, but I think you've been enemy indoctrinated, and you don't even know it.

Person1: This ain't gonna work.
Person2: Rae...
Person1: I don't see why we gotta lie 'bout it when you and I know this ain't gonna work.
Person2: Rae don't do this now...
Person1: It's stupid... It's so stupid!
Person2: Rae!

Person1: Marlon and Al....
Person2: We want them.  You will not let anyone go into that house--or anything come out-- until my people recover these disks.
Person1: I can't control what happens.  The Sheriffs are running the scene.
Person2: You will re-assume command.  In two hours, a group of my people will arrive at York Estates.  You will tell the Sheriffs that they are an FBI tactical team.

Person1: Don't, Beth.
Person2: But I have to, Norman.
Person1: I promise --
Person2: Promise what, Norman?  Tell me!
Person1: Beth --
Person2: You think I'd belive you?  Believe anything you might say now?  After you lied to me about telling Barnes about my past.  About my problems.  My problems.  Not yours.  Not his.  Mine.
Person1: Don't, Beth.  Please.
Person2: It's either this... or I kill you.

Person1: Having fun yet?  No?  I'll give you a hint.  Remember whatshername?
Person2: Shelly?
Person1: Miss her?
Person2: Yes.
Person1: Kill the men who killed you both, and the Day of the Dead will be your reunion.

Person1: I just can't deal with all this crap between us, I'm sorry.  I'm quitting.
Person2: Let's get hitched.  I guess I, you know, love you.  It's a beautiful thing.
Person1: Wha --

Person1: Miss Kreutz, please... I'm gonna have to ask you to keep your voice down.
Person2: All the papers -- all the papers they asked for -- I brought all the papers --
Person1: Miss Kreutz, excuse me, but you entered into a fraudulent marriage in an effort to circumvent the immigration laws of the United States --
Person2: You only know that because I told you!  Ask the case officer -- find his name -- it's on the papers -- I told him all this myself! --
Person1: -- it's not the source of the information that's important here --
Person2: -- I paid this guy -- I paid him four thousand dollars -- my last four thousand dollars to marry me, okay? -- I told this to the case officer last week...  ...here -- Mr. Thomas.  I told Mr. Thomas I didn't know this guy was already married -- I admitted this!
Person1: -- Miss Kreutz, please --
Person2: -- I'm the one that got ripped off! -- not you -- not the United States government -- me -- I'm the one being ripped off!
Person1: So now you're asking for a student visa?

Person1: Game's over. Last chance to get out of this without a prison sentence.  You're not good enough to beat me, you little shit.
Person2: Yeah, maybe I'm not. But we are, you asshole.
Person1: Give it up! Just give it up.

Person1: You were quick enough to get Tom's help when...
Person2: Yes, yes.  I know.  Right.  And if it had gone well for me tonight, maybe I'd be keeping quiet about all this...I grant you everything but give me this...he does personify everything you've been fighting against...And I'm in love with you.  How do you like that? -- I buried the lead.

Person1: Never play with someone's else's cards. You always get a new deck. Look at this card. See anything?
Person2: It is just a regular card.
Person1: Son, I can take everything you got with that deck. It is marked. Now I'm going to show you how to make some money in case you get stuck somewhere.

Person1: Misery's Return?
Person2: I know you didn't mean it when you killed her, and now you'll make it right.

Person1: You walk out that door and you lose your share of the money.
Person2: Yeah, whatever.
Person1: I mean it!
Person2: Adios.

Person1: That shirt, Mr. Boone.
Person2: It's new.
Person1: I'm sorry.  It's too white, too distracting.  Would it be asking too much for you to take it off?
Person2: I'm not wearing an undershirt.
Person1: Pish posh, Mr. Boone.  I'm not your Aunt Tilly.
Person2: But it's just my face you want to draw.
Person1: Oh if it's going to make you uncomfortable...  Perhaps we can find something else for you to wear.

Person1: I swear to you, Argon, if you don't stop the meltdown that nanobot will be the last one I ever build.
Person2: Susan, I sense you are having difficulty understanding the situation you are presently in.  I ask that you keep in mind that I am ready to reduce an entire city to gelatin to get what I want.

Person1: It's the same questions, Mrs. Little. Since the death of your husband --
Person2: Murder.
Person1: -- there is a serious question as to whether --
Person2: These are my children. Mine. And they ain't no question. None.
Person1: I think sometimes, Mrs. Little, candor is the only kindness.
Person1: All of your children are delinquent, Mrs. Little, and one, at least, Malcolm is a thief.
Person2: Get out.
Person1: Your control over your children, therefore --
Person2: Did you hear me?!
Person1: You'll regret this, Mrs. Little.
Person2: If you don't move out through that door, you're going to be past all regretting.

Person1: ... So me and Sounder, ran right by the men that was the prisoners, and crawled under the fence! That's when I cut my hand on the glass. It hurt like mad! But I kept runnin', and then I didn't feel no more hurtin' till I stopped runnin'!  Why was that, Miss Johnson?
Person2: Because all you could think of was getting away from the guard.
Person1: Oh... . You right, that's all that was worryin' me right then...
Person2: You know, that was a good thing you did for Clarence, in class today. I believed his story, but I was afraid the other children wouldn't...
Person1: He was real scared nobody would believe him -- I could tell, even when he first stood up.
Person2: You could? How?
Person1: I been like that myself, when I thought nobody was gonna believe me...  You wanna hear another story?!
Person2: Not now, David, it's time for bed. You have a long trip ahead of you in the morning...

Person1: Yes.
Person2: You have what I asked for?
Person1: I do.
Person2: Pack it into black garbage bags. Throw the bags into a garbage dump in the alley behind the Fang Shan Restaurant in China Town.  You have twenty-nine minutes left.

Person1: She's in love.
Person2: You're crazy.  I think you're in love.

Person1: Now that is a happy sound! Tomorrow, I want the citizens -- my people -- back in the arena.  The Gods know, I'm tired.  Come to bed, now; tonight we're celebrating.
Person2: What are you talking about?
Person1: Now that we're done with that infatuation forever.
Person2: If I ever loved Narcissus it wasn't like you want.
Person1: But I get what I want, always, don't I?

Person1: Promise you what?
Person2: That you're not crazy.
Person1: I promise you that. I am not crazy. Course, if I was, I'd be the last to know. Why do you ask?
Person2: You talk to yourself.
Person1: But I don't listen...then I'd be crazy.
Person2: Why do you do it?
Person1: If I was with you, I wouldn't
Person2: You did.
Person1: That was then. This is now. Who am I talking to?
Person2: You're talking to me.
Person1: And how'm I doing?
Person2: You're doing...Okay.
Person1: Just Okay...

Person1: You lied to us, Levi, you're going to the gas chamber unless you tell us where to find them!
Person2: I don't know --
Person1: Where are they!
Person2: Maybe --
Person1: MAYBE WHAT --
Person2: Maybe he...

Person1: Your blood.
Person2: All my life I have never reached a man. I knew that I would reach my husband, but I took my time and he was dead. Ronny.
Person1: Loretta.
Person2: I want to cut you open and crawl inside of you. I want you to swallow me.
Person1: I've got you.
Person2: Get all of me. Take everything.
Person1: What about Johnny?
Person2: You're mad at him, take it out on me, take your revenge on me! Take everything, leave nothing for him to marry! Hollow me out so there's nothing left but the skin over my bones. Suck me dry!
Person1: Alright. Alright. There will be nothing left.

Person1: Who are you?
Person2: My name's Lydia.  And it seems you and I have a lot in common.
Person1: You said you knew where my fiance was the other night.
Person2: With me, where he's been after work, three, sometimes four nights a week for the last two months... We have what you might call an intimate relationship.
Person1: How do I know you're telling the truth?
Person2: He been sleeping in a T-shirt lately?  That's so you won't see the fingernail marks on his back.  Bet you didn't know your boyfriend finds a little pain exciting.  He didn't either...at first.
Person1: Look, I don't know what you want--
Person2: I thought it was important for you to know the facts.
Person1: And so should you.  Sketchy told me I could expect a visit from you.  I know all about how you threatened him.  That if he didn't break it off with me, you'd save him the trouble.
Person2: Oh?
Person1: Well, it's over between you and him. We're getting married next month.
Person2: How sweet.  Standing by your man, even after what he did.  You're a very understanding person.

Person1: Am I?  I say you came running after me because you were afraid I was leaving you without even a kiss.

It's a shame, because you've got all the makings for one.  I could have helped you plenty in that department ... if you'd have let go for a minute.  But it's too late now, sweetheart.  Your big opportunity is flying out of here.

Person1: Ann, in life one has to be aware of hidden agendas.  Did it occur to you that Graham may have his own reasons for not wanting you to be in therapy?
Person2: What do you mean? I don't understand.
Person1: It's possible that Graham has hidden motives for disliking therapy and/or therapists. Perhaps he has problems of his own that he is unwilling to deal with, and he would like to see other people, you for instance, wallow in their situation just as he does. Do you think that's possible?
Person2: I guess.
Person1: You understand that you are free to leave therapy at any time?
Person2: Yes.
Person1: That you are under no obligation to me?
Person2: Yes.
Person1: Do you want to leave therapy?
Person2: Not really.
Person1: Do you feel there is more progress to be made?
Person2: Yes.
Person1: I'm glad you feel that way, because I feel that way, too.
Person2: But you don't have hidden motives for feeling that way, right?

Person1: Are you crazy, a lift raft?! We're not sinking, we're crashing!

Person1: You're making this very hard. I'll be fine. Really.
Person2: I don't think so. They've got you in a glass jar like some butterfly, and you're goin' to die if you don't break out. Maybe not right away, 'cause you're strong. But sooner or later the fire in you is goin' to go out.
Person1: It's not up to you to save me, Jack.
Person2: You're right. Only you can do that.
Person1: I have to get back, they'll miss me. Please, Jack, for both our sakes, leave me alone.

Person1: Oh, I was going to discover the secret of the universe.  That's why I liked Nix.  He promised me all these explanations.
Person2: And he didn't have them?
Person1: He had something.  He showed me how to bend the rules.  A little levitation.  A few fireworks.
Person2: Is that all?
Person1: No.  At the end... when we had him cornered, he got into my head. He showed me what we really look like, when the veneer's gone.  Jelly. Shit...
Person2: And you believed him?
Person1: I saw it with my own eyes! See, that's his best trick.  No illusions.  Just the truth.  Are you ready for that?

Person1: Yeah, I had a small problem with members of our side murdering civil-...
Person1: ...-ians.  But I assure you, Leo...
Person1: ...I lost all my squeamishness at Leav- ...
Person1: ...-enworth.  I'll have no compunction at all about using this.

Person1: ...maybe even Salt Lake, not to mention Casper...
Person1: ...Destry, Fair Oaks....

Person2: ...here!
Person1: And I'm warning you, Leo.  Don't test me. Get your men and your machines off my radar screen in five, or three million people will die.
Person1: I'll do it.

Person1: What'd they give you?  Thorazine?  How much?  Learn your drugs -- know your doses.
Person2: I need to make a telephone call.
Person1: A telephone call?  That's communication with the outside world!  Doctor's discretion.  Hey, if alla these nuts could just make phone calls, it could spread.  Insanity oozing through telephone cables, oozing into the ears of all those poor sane people, infecting them!  Whackos everywhere!  A plague of madness.  In fact, very few of us here are actually mentally ill.  I'm not saying you're not mentally ill, for all I know you're crazy as a loon.  But that's not why you're here.  Why you're here is because of the system, because of the economy.  There's the TV.  It's all right there. Commercials.  We are not productive anymore, they don't need us to make things anymore, it's all automated.  What are we for then?  We're consumers.  Okay, buy a lot of stuff, you're a good citizen. But if you don't buy a lot of stuff, you know what?  You're mentally ill! That's a fact!  If you don't buy things...toilet paper, new cars, computerized blenders, electrically operated sexual devices...  SCREWDRIVERS WITH MINIATURE BUILT-IN RADAR DEVICES, STEREO SYSTEMS WITH BRAIN IMPLANTED HEADPHONES, VOICE- ACTIVATED COMPUTERS, AND...

Person1: We think you should give up the magazine. Sell it. Let someone else run it. Whatever it takes.
Person2: We want you to go to college, find a career you love, marry a nice girl from a nice family, and live happily ever after.

Person1: This is the mortgage.  This is Cindy's college money.
Person2: I understand.

Person2: I know.

Person2: Why the gun?
Person1: I'm not gonna need it.  I won't even wear it.  It's a precaution.  Don't worry about me.

Person1: Jon, I do not want to hear it.
Person2: I'm sorry.  Look -- there's a gas storm on the Valles Marineris . . .

Person2: How're the tips?

Person2: I'll need it--gave my last buck to the bedroom steward.

Person2: Come on--  I'll promote a card game, and we'll be sitting pretty.
Person1: Until then--the dollar forty stays with me.
Person2: Is that a nice way to talk to a pal?
Person1: Don't let money spoil a beautiful friendship. Look at these here telegrams to be delivered! This ain't a job-- it's a marathon.

Person1: We have to think this through.  We have to be smart.  Now is not the time to panic.  If we panic now, we're done for.  Nobody saw it. Nobody knows it was us.  We're still okay.  Now all we have to do is just figure out our next move.
Person2: Our next move?  I'll say one thing, Louise.  This is some vacation.  I sure am having a good time.  This is real fun.

Person2: Just what is that supposed to mean?
Person1: It means shut up, Thelma.
Person2: So this is all my fault, is it.

Person1: Stu? Is that you? Where did you go?

Person1: This was my first classroom, John, did you know that?  My first desk.

I'm not saying they have anything to do with the Dalton boy's outburst, but I don't think I have to warn you that boys his age are very impressionable.

Person1: You'll catch cold. It's cold.  What do you suppose he is, a survivalist?

Person2: He looks perfectly fine.
Person1: I think we should go. Please. Before he wakes up and, I don't know, eats us, or whatever feral things do.
Person2: I don't understand you. This is fascinating and you just want to run away. I mean, here we have a human being totally uncontaminated by civilization, totally free, and all you want to do is run back to your...

Person2: What?

Person2: Nathan, what the hell are you talking about?
Person1: Forget mice! Actually forget guinea pigs, cats, monkeys, and chimps also. I'm on to stage five: The human subject.
Person2: Oh no. You can't take him from his home, Nathan.
Person1: Don't you see? He's my Tabula Rasa, my Eliza Dolittle. He's my ticket to the top of the Behaviorist food chain. He's going to make me famous.
Person2: I won't allow you. It's wrong. He's happy here.
Person1: Is he, Lila? Is he happy living filthy and naked alone in this tick infested wilderness? Never to know the love of a good woman, never to revel in the pitter-patter of little feet, never to read Moby Dick, or marvel at a Monet, or just sit back after a day of hard but rewarding work, smoke a pipe, and wonder about the nature of reality.

Person1: It doesn't look much like the fish spears I knew back in Marblehead..
Person2: Our friend, Poseidon, didn't use it for fishing. He raked the sea with it and stirred up the big waves.

Person1: Well
Person2: Go ahead. I'll wake you up when I go to bed. Then you can watch him.

Person1: Let me explain about time- you've been here three months and already two months behind. And the Germans and the French are gearing up. And I don't care about you and I don't care about the thirty dead- I care about my knighthood and if this railroad finishes on schedule, I'll get my knighthood and I want it.  Professional hunters may be the answer.
Person2: All they'll bring is more chaos and we've plenty of that already- and if they come in, word will get out- and what happens to your knighthood then?
Person1: I'm going to try and locate Redbeard- I assume you've heard of him.
Person2: Every man who's ever fired a rifle has heard of him- by the time you find him, the lions will be dead.

Person1: Hey, what do you say we call off this little charade of yours over a couple of beers at Trader Jon's...? Come on, man. You're about as close to being officer material as me.

Person1: That's not how she told it. She said you wrote her every week you were away.

Person2: I never said any of that!

Person2: Okay, I wrote those things... and yeah, I had big thoughts of getting together with your mom... but when she hit me with being pregnant, I saw who she was. I'd had quiff lay that shit on me before!

Person1: Why not?
Person2: Oh come on, Kathryn. It's too easy. "But I thought high school was only four years." I mean, please. She knows nothing. She's seen nothing. I could have her under the table at Au Bar sucking me off before the appetizer arrived. Go get one of those moron friends of yours to do it. I have a reputation to uphold.

Person2: That was just simple revenge. What I have planned requires sheer genius.

Person1: HEY!!!
Person2: Show's over unless I get to see what you've been working on after hours.

Person2: Do I get to see it or not?
Person1: Bastard...  Sheila, don't go anywhere. I'll be right back.

Person1: You killed them.
Person2: It was only a matter of time before somebody killed somebody.  You saw it coming as well as I did. Dodge had his plans, starting with scuttling the boat. And Greer too, except he went nuts. Couldn't take it, I guess. Could've happened in the middle of downtown Anchorage. But did it make him any less dangerous? I don't think so.
Person1: So you killed them?
Person2: The way I figure it, it was them or me. I thought putting Dodge up on that pipe was a nice touch? Bought a little time. Made it look like whoever killed those Greeks was still around. But it's just us on this ship. Us and your... spirit friends.
Person1: And now you're gonna kill me, is that it?
Person2: I didn't want it to turn out this way.
Person1: Murphy, don't you see what's happening?
Person2: I think I see it pretty well.
Person1: It's the ship. The ship's making you think this way.
Person2: I know a little bit about human nature and what I've seen only confirms that.
Person1: It's a trap. There was no way we were gonna get away with that gold. Nobody ever does. It's just the bait. This ship sucks people in and it never lets them out.
Person2: I think maybe you been on this boat a little too long, with all that supernatural mumbo jumbo. There's nothing supernatural about greed. And that's what it comes down to, pure and simple.
Person1: I don't give a damn about the gold.
Person2: I wish I could believe that. Either way, you know what I've done. I've got no choice.

Person1: Come on. We got a couple hours to eat and get some sleep.
Person2: I'll eat when I'm good and ready.
Person1: Kind of feeling your oats this morning. I haven't laid a hand on you for quite a while, but that doesn't mean you're too old.
Person2: What makes you think you're so almighty? Telling people what to do and how to act when you don't even know how yourself.

Person1: Addison wants me to play it.
Person2: Over my dead body...
Person1: That won't be necessary. Addison knows how Margo happen to miss that performance - how I happened to know she'd miss it in time to call him and notify every paper in town...  ... it's quite a story. Addison could make quite a thing of it - imagine how snide and vicious he could get and still write nothing but the truth. I had a time persuading him...  ... you'd better sit down. You look a bit wobbly.  If I play "Cora," Addison will never tell what happened - in or out of print. A simple exchange of favors. And I'm so happy I can do something for you - at long last...  Your friendship with Margo - your deep, close friendship - what would happen to it, do you think, if she knew the chap trick you'd played on her - for my benefit? And you and Lloyd - how long, even in the Theater, before people forgot what happened - and trusted you again?  No... it would be so much easier on everyone concerned, if I were to play "Cora." And so much better theater, too...

Person1: I don't suppose there's a drink left...
Person2: You can have one at Max's.

Person2: It's the same thing, isn't it?
Person1: Exactly.  Here. Take it to the party instead of me.

Person2: I thought it was the right thing to do. It was complete.

Person2: It wasn't like that. I just wanted to be efficient.

Person1: You need to come down here, everybody meets everybody.  Ho ho ho.  Ha ha ha. We do one for good faith and then we talk about airplanes.
Person2: I can't go anywhere, Diego.  I'm on parole.  I can't leave the state.
Person1: But you must.  It's the only way.
Person2: I just got released five minutes ago.

Person1: There's nothing we can do.  It's just a matter of time before they unlock this room.  There goes my model.  There goes our last refuge.
Person2: We're not going to wait here like cornered animals.  I can tell you that.  We need help.  I'm going to talk to that little girl.
Person1: What about this Beetle guy?
Person2: We don't know who he is...  ... I'm going to talk to that little girl.
Person1: Are you crazy?  She can't hear you.
Person2: I don't know... what are you looking up?
Person1: We need some help.  I found something this morning.  Here. Emergencies.  'In case of emergency, draw door.'
Person2: Draw door?  I don't know why we keep looking in that stupid book.

Person1: Look, I just thought if you finally got rid of this obsession with this name, if you could get it out of your system once and for all, then maybe you'd be able to stop waiting and open your heart to someone real.  Granted, ideally, me.  I didn't do it to hurt you.  I did it for you.  I did it for us...
Person2: There is no us.

Person1: Honey, Rose was born on a farm and has terrible memories of farm life. Now I don't think --
Person2: Just a minute, Rose, you don't understand. This isn't a dirt farm like the one you were born on, it's nice. A neighbor of Cousin Hop's, I talked to him on the phone, a fine man... it's not a farm, damn it, it's a beautiful dairy establishment! Stop crying, Rose! Do you hear me? Stop crying, it's ideal!
Person1: I don't know how you can call it ideal -- don't you know what the word "farm" means to her?
Person2: I don't give a hoot in hell's hollow what it means to her! She can't stay in this house forever! I fired her, damn it!
Person1: It sounds like a farm to me. Calling it a dairy establishment, that's just trickery and flummery.
Person2: Trickery and flummery or not, she's going there tomorrow on the bus! It's settled! And I must say we've been more than fair to you, Rose, it's pretty selfish and mean hearted of you to sit there and cry like that!

Person1: Why not?

Person1: You're all packed.  And here's your bus ticket. You're on your way back to St. Anne's.
Person2: Pop, I'm not ready to go back yet.
Person1: Edie, for years we pushed quarters into a cookie jar, to keep you up there with the Sisters, and to keep you from things like I just seen out the window. My own daughter arm-in-arm with Terry Malloy. You know who Terry Malloy is?
Person2: Who is he, Pop?
Person1: Who is he! Edie, you're so softhearted and soft-headed you wouldn't recognize the devil if he had you by the throat. You know who this Terry Malloy is? The kid brother of Charlie the Gent, Johnny Friendly's right hand, a butcher in a camel hair coat.
Person2: Are you trying to tell me Terry is too?
Person1: I'm not trying to tell you he's Little Lord Fauntleroy.
Person2: He tries to act tough, but there's a look in his eyes that... .
Person1: A look in his eyes! Hold your hats, brother, here we go again. You think he's one of those cases you're always draggin' in and feelin' sorry for. Like the litter of kittens you hadthe only one she wants to keep has six toes and it's cockeyed to boot. Look at him. The bum! And the crush you had on that little Abyssinian... .
Person2: He wasn't Abyssinian, Pop, Assyrian... .
Person1: Six-toed cats. Assyrians. Abyssinians. It's the same difference. Well don't think this Terry Malloy is any six-toed cockeyed Assyrian. He's a bum. Charley and Johnny Friendly owned him when he was a fighter and when they ring the bell he still goes into action.
Person2: He wanted to see me again.
Person1: You think we kept you out in Tarrytown just to have you go walkin' with a corner saloon hoodlum like Terry Malloy? Now get back to Tarrytown, before I put a strap to you.
Person2: And learn about charity and justice and all the other things people would rather talk about than practice? Pop goes up to her and holds out his two arms, his right one closer to Edie; he trembles with emotion.
Person1: See this arm? It's two inches longer 'n the other one. That's years of workin' and sweatin', liftin' and swingin' a hook. And every time I heisted a box or a coffee bag I says to myselfthis is for Edie, so she can be a teacher or somethin' decent. I promised your mother. You better not let her down.

Person1: All right, sugar, what's on your mind?
Person2: Don't look at me like that. Auggie. It gives me the creeps.
